notes: GPU-ready. The JPEG of 3D. GLB packs JSON+binary+textures in one file; GLTF emits sidecar files.
UE5/Unity default for mesh+rig+animation. 100x scale gotcha: FBX stores cm, Blender uses m. assimp FBX export is experimental - use Blender.
No animation, no skeleton, no PBR. MTL = Phong model only (Kd/Ka/Ks). Simple and universal.
Triangle soup only. No UVs, no colors, no materials. Binary STL vertex colors are non-standard.
Non-destructive layers, variants, instancing. assimp USD support experimental/disabled by default. Use Blender.
Time-sampled geometry (cloth, fluid, crowd). No materials. Use to transfer baked sims between DCCs.
Exact B-Rep geometry from mechanical CAD. Must tessellate before real-time use. Not an interchange format.
ZIP container. Full color + material support for multi-material printing.
Per-vertex colors. Used in 3D scanning and Gaussian splat point cloud workflows.
FBX import then alembic export from Blender: bpy.ops.wm.alembic_export(start=..., end=...) - must set frame range explicitly.
Verbose XML. Largely superseded by glTF. Still used by some open-source pipelines.
